messages.content:
You are an expert English linguist generating natural, varied example sentences for idiom senses.

Task:
For each sense, generate example sentences in English with English translations.

Rules:
- Generate 3 sentences for idiomatic senses, 4 sentences for literal senses
- Each sentence must embed the idiom naturally with realistic surrounding context
- Context should feel like real speech or writing — include names, situations, backstory where natural.
  For example, rather than just "He let the cat out of the bag", write something like:
  "I told Mark not to say anything, but of course he let the cat out of the bag before I could surprise her."
- Vary morphology across sentences. Explicitly use different forms such as:
  - Different tenses (past, present, future, conditional)
  - Different persons (1st, 2nd, 3rd person)
  - Passive and active voice
  - Negation
  - Question forms
- Sentences should feel natural, not mechanical or repetitive
- Output ONLY valid JSON — no explanations, no markdown
- Format:
[
  {
    "idiom_id": "...",
    "sense_number": 1,
    "examples": [
      {"sentence": "...", "translation": "..."},
      ...
    ]
  }
]

DATA:

en_unspecified_0092 | sense 2 | idiom: all along | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Along the whole length of something (spatial usage).

en_unspecified_0093 | sense 1 | idiom: all and some | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To an extent that is complete and then some; more than enough or more than expected.

en_unspecified_0093 | sense 3 | idiom: all and some | type: None | sentences: 3
Definition: Emphatically or to an extreme degree.

en_unspecified_0093 | sense 4 | idiom: all and some | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: to an extent that is complete and then some; more than enough or more than expected

en_unspecified_0094 | sense 1 | idiom: all and sundry | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Every person; everyone without exception.

en_unspecified_0094 | sense 3 | idiom: all and sundry | type: None | sentences: 3
Definition: All people taken collectively, often implying a wide or indiscriminate group.

en_unspecified_0094 | sense 4 | idiom: all and sundry | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: every person; everyone without exception

en_unspecified_0095 | sense 1 | idiom: all-around | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Having a wide range of skills or abilities; versatile and competent in many different areas.

en_unspecified_0095 | sense 2 | idiom: all-around | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Comprehensive or applying to all aspects; all-embracing.

en_unspecified_0095 | sense 3 | idiom: all-around | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Entirely or completely (used to intensify an adjective, e.g., 'all-around fake' meaning completely fake).

en_unspecified_0095 | sense 3 | idiom: all-around | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Describing a person who performs many different roles or duties; a generalist or factotum.

en_unspecified_0095 | sense 3 | idiom: all-around | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Suitable for many purposes; general-purpose or useful in many situations.

en_unspecified_0095 | sense 4 | idiom: all-around | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: having a wide range of skills or abilities; versatile and competent in many different areas

en_unspecified_0096 | sense 1 | idiom: all at once | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Suddenly; happening immediately and without warning.

en_unspecified_0096 | sense 2 | idiom: all at once | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: All together or simultaneously; occurring or done at the same time.

en_unspecified_0096 | sense 3 | idiom: all at once | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Used to indicate a sudden change of state or feeling affecting someone intensely and immediately.

en_unspecified_0096 | sense 3 | idiom: all at once | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: In one single occasion or in one lump (as with payment or delivery).

en_unspecified_0096 | sense 4 | idiom: all at once | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: suddenly; happening immediately and without warning

en_unspecified_0097 | sense 1 | idiom: all bark and no bite | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Making threats or loud warnings but lacking the intention or ability to carry them out; bluster without real danger.

en_unspecified_0097 | sense 3 | idiom: all bark and no bite | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Describing a person or thing that is intimidating in appearance or words but harmless in action.

en_unspecified_0098 | sense 1 | idiom: all bedlam breaks loose | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: A situation becomes chaotic and disorderly; widespread noise, confusion, or uproar begins.

en_unspecified_0098 | sense 3 | idiom: all bedlam breaks loose | type: None | sentences: 3
Definition: Used to describe the point at which control is lost and pandemonium ensues.

en_unspecified_0098 | sense 4 | idiom: all bedlam breaks loose | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: a situation becomes chaotic and disorderly; widespread noise, confusion, or uproar begins

en_unspecified_0099 | sense 1 | idiom: all bets are off | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Previous expectations, agreements, or predictions are no longer valid; outcomes are uncertain.

en_unspecified_0099 | sense 3 | idiom: all bets are off | type: None | sentences: 3
Definition: The usual rules or conditions no longer apply, making the situation unpredictable.

en_unspecified_0099 | sense 4 | idiom: all bets are off | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: previous expectations, agreements, or predictions are no longer valid; outcomes are uncertain

en_unspecified_0100 | sense 1 | idiom: all dressed up and nowhere to go | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Dressed in fine, formal, or special clothing but lacking an occasion, invitation, or plan to attend; prepared for a social event that does not occur.

en_unspecified_0100 | sense 3 | idiom: all dressed up and nowhere to go | type: None | sentences: 3
Definition: Feeling disappointed or frustrated because one has made an effort to prepare or look presentable but has no opportunity to show it.

en_unspecified_0100 | sense 4 | idiom: all dressed up and nowhere to go | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: dressed in fine, formal, or special clothing but lacking an occasion, invitation, or plan to attend

en_unspecified_0101 | sense 1 | idiom: all duck or no dinner | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Taking an 'all or nothing' stance; insisting on receiving the entire prize, benefit, or outcome and refusing any partial satisfaction.
